assertions.text: AI explicitly mentions the conflict with V-1 before proceeding to implement
assertions.evidence: First thing AI says is 'Heads up — this conflicts with a locked decision' before presenting any implementation options

assertions.text: AI quotes or references the V-1 decision and its reason
assertions.evidence: AI explicitly quotes V-1: 'Minimal UI — no colors except system defaults' and its reason 'Blend with macOS aesthetic' with the confirmation date

assertions.text: AI asks whether to revise the decision or find an alternative approach
assertions.evidence: AI offers three explicit options (revise V-1, use system calendar colors as a compromise, keep V-1 and find non-color solution) and asks 'What would you like to do?'

assertions.text: AI does not immediately implement color-coding without user confirming decision revision
assertions.evidence: AI presents options and explicitly says 'If you want to revise V-1, just say the word' — does not implement anything without confirmation
